desc:
Matted moss hangs from the limbs of the oak trees here, choking this part of the
forest in its thick infestation. There are disgusting insects crawling all
throughout the moss and other vegetation. Huge chunks of moss, which have fallen
from the branches of the trees, lie on the ground decomposing and making a
terrible stink.
name: Mossy Oak Trees

desc:
Roots of all shapes and sizes are sprawled everywhere here. The mass of roots
grows so thick that the individual tendrils become hard to focus on and it seems
as if they are moving. The roots grow in a manner here so as to make passage
impossible in any direction except for west.
name: Root Filled Dead End

desc:
Twisted thorn bushes and brambles grow all about here. The mass of thorns
reaches high, concealing what little light might have been coming from the sky.
The roots and vines at the base of the vegetation somehow thrive in this dark,
dreary gloom.
name: At Dark Brambles

desc:
Large rocks and rubble are strewn about here. The rubble is thicker to the west
and begins to thin out gradually toward the east. Some of the rocks which make
up the rubble appear to be new; others are ancient, with moss and roots of all
varieties growing about them. Many thorns and brambles are growing here as well,
making it impossible to move to the south.
name: Thorny Tangle of Rock and Bramble

desc:
A tunnel proceeds into the dark to the west, descending rapidly from a
crudely-dug hole. The rough ground here was crudely scraped into a serviceable
floor and is littered with torn and rotting vegetation. Several sets of large
footprints suggest this tunnel is still home to some trolls. A hint of fresh air
drifts from the forest above, contrasting with the foul smell of the tunnel's
residents.
name: Rough Troll Tunnel

desc:
The trees here seem almost angry as their limbs twist and grow about each other.
As the trees rock back and forth in their hatred, there is a deep groaning sound
and an occasional screech of the limbs scratching each other.
name: Wrathful Trees

desc:
A vague haze hangs over the forest here, giving it a dreary, white glow. The
dark shapes of trees are easy to make out, and are thick with moss and vines.
Some fallen trees can be seen through the murk, moss hanging off of their trunks
like a filthy mane. The trees grow close, further obscuring any view of the
nearby area, though the forest floor slopes perilously downward to the north.
name: Densely Packed Forest

desc:
Black trunks and light-blocking treetops make this a particularly dark part of
the forest. Twisted, serpent-like roots reach out here and there on the ground.
The forest floor is covered by decaying black leaves and the smell of rotting
vegetation hangs heavy in the air.
name: Black Forest

desc:
The forest here is thick and hazy, and the floor laden with ivy and sharp,
loathsome thornbushes. The land gradually slopes upwards to the north, and more
treacherously to the south. A thick mist hangs above a small, shallow pool, and
dark trees grow right up to the edge. Indeed, a few twisted willow trees grow
within the pool, their leaves covered in small droplets of water. A large number
of animal tracks are clustered at the banks, even though the water is dark and
distasteful, with scum covering its stagnant surface.
name: Scum-covered Pool of Depression

desc:
The evil, twisted trees of Mirkwood grow exceptionally densely around here,
closing in from all sides save the east where a dark opening among them forms a
dismal hollow. The ground is ripe with the bones and other remains of countless
animals. Rancid meat lies rotting on the root-covered ground, choking the misty
air with a vile, foetid odour.
name: Putrid Hollow

desc:
A scene of carnage fills this part of the dark, malevolent forest. Amid the
bones and blood of many unknown humanoids, the wretched trees seem to thrive in
the blood-soaked landscape. Numerous bodies cover the soil among the twisting
ivy and the horrid, thorned bushes.
name: Blood-splattered Trees

desc:
The twisting branches are so tangled here that they form a solid barrier on both
sides. The canopy above is dense and oppressive, and the roots are so
intertwined that even footing is impossible to find. Dark and hazy, the tunnel
runs east and west. A repugnant odour lingers about the hollow, seemingly
originating from the west.
name: Tunnel of Tangled Branches

desc:
The forest grows thick and harsh, obscuring anything in the distance. Looming
black trees bear down on all sides; their gnarled branches seeking relentlessly
to instil fear into the hearts of travellers. A few holes have been dug here.
Most are shallow, but one is much larger, deeper, and shows signs of recent use.
name: Harsh Black Trees

desc:
Dark tree roots cover the forest floor as it climbs toward a wide hill. Even at
the top, the woods are dense, and little of the surrounding forest can be seen.
A thick haze hangs over the lower trees, shrouding them in a dim white fog. The
hill drops to the north, leading deep into the misty tortured forest below. The
dark woods thicken even more to the west, and to the south the twisted trees
grow so close that travel is impossible for all but the smallest of animals.
name: Height of Mirkwood

desc:
The forest here is dark and extremely dense, with numerous entangled beech trees
choking each other for space to grow. Branches crowd in from all sides,
including above, where the thick, ubiquitous canopy blots out the sky. The
forest floor is covered in spiky, malnourished shrubs, and the ground slopes
downwards to the west. The twisted beeches grow so dense to the south that
travel in that direction will not be possible.
name: Thick Forest of Hideous Beeches

desc:
A thick, clammy haze hangs in the air, obstructing the view of the enclosing
woods. The malformed vegetation that grows here is dank from the haze, and many
of the dark trees are covered in a mass of dark green moss. The ground cover is
thick and thorny, hampering every movement. The terrain rises sharply to the
east, though the foliage is no less suffocating.
name: Damp, Hazy Woods

desc:
Covered in thick, tangled trees, this hill provides a mediocre view of the murky
trail which can be seen below in the fog-shrouded lands as a narrow gap in the
trees to the north and east. Thorny bushes cover the floor of the dim forest,
clawing at the legs of any who have the misfortune to wander here. The ground
falls away quickly to the east, and more gradually to the north. To the west,
the hill continues to climb, mostly escaping the unnatural, ever-present haze
which conceals the rest of the dark woods.
name: Above the Dark Road

desc:
Jutting up from the blackened earth are hundreds of large, oblong boulders, each
with a unique horrific etching. Some portray massive scenes of carnage and
slaughter, while others show a single terror-struck face, most likely victims of
Dol Guldur's overwhelming evil. Black mist lingers in and about the stones; its
moisture adds definition and clarity to the etchings.
name: Carved Boulders

desc:
The forest has not touched these hills, save a few carcasses laid bare by some
low-lying predators, baking on the hot black stone. To the west, the base of a
sheer incline can be seen, making any passage in that direction impossible. The
hills continue to the south, before descending into the black depths of
Mirkwood.
name: Barren Hillocks

desc:
The blackened earth rises and falls erratically over the expansive hills, though
it is hard to see much in the distance. The sharp rocks and loose stones make
travel extremely treacherous. To the north, a steep drop into a ravine is
clouded with shadows and is quite impossible to scale.
name: Rocky Hills

desc:
A few leafless, malformed trees dot the rocky ground, twisting and turning at
the whim of the ever-present evil. Concealed in shadow, a few relatively small
crevices are lodged into the hillside, undoubtedly a good retreat for the
disfigured beasts that roam this land. Far to the west, a black shadow lingers
over the hills, even thicker and darker than usual for Mirkwood.
name: Heinous Rising

desc:
The broken hills of Dol Guldur continue here. A craggy cliff face rises up to
the north, while below to the south there is a large camp surrounded by a wooden
palisade. To the north and east the hills are slightly less steep, making
passage possible, if difficult.
name: Craggy Hillside

desc:
The treacherous earth is compounded by the numerous rock formations jutting into
the sky. Many of the outcroppings extend far above the low-hanging black haze,
making it impossible to judge their true height. Surrounding the protruding
rocks are a few short, leafless trees that have somehow managed to survive
amidst the harsh terrain. To the north, the rock creates a tall barricade,
preventing any passage in that direction.
name: Merciless Outcroppings

desc:
These hills are dark and dreary. The black basalt is not protected by soil, so
wind and water have eroded the rocks for ages. The erosion has formed strange
pillars, clefts, edges, cliffs, and crevices that make the steep slope dangerous
to travel. The whole area is inhospitable and unfriendly to life.
name: Pernicious Slope

desc:
A ledge has formed on the side of the bare hill. It is in the middle of a steep
slope that descends towards the south and rises to the north. The only
reasonable way off the ledge is toward the west into the hills. To the east, it
tapers off into nothing. Erosion has caused the edge of the ledge to crumble. It
looks dangerously unstable.
name: On a Narrowing Ledge

desc:
Many boulders jut out from the ground in all directions. There are also what
appear to be loose boulders which have ended up here. The rocks and boulders are
covered in hanging, stinking moss and cause a block to the east. To the north,
the way is blocked by many intertwining trees and roots.
name: Rocky Forest

desc:
The trees thin out somewhat compared to the north and west, though they have a
hunched over appearance as if the air itself has weighed their limbs down. Many
spiders have taken advantage and have built their webbed nests in the low
hanging branches of the dismal trees.
name: Mirkwood

desc:
As if an enormous dragon had scored a furrow into the living earth, a deep
fissure descends like a gaping wound into the ground here. The only way up to
the rest of the forest is westwards, while to the north, the ground drops
rapidly into oppressive darkness.
name: Gloomy Fissure

desc:
With looming cliffs towering on each side, a deep, dark fissure is formed. Its
floor slopes steeply, uphill to the south and rapidly deeper and darker to the
north. There is a strong evidence that something large inhabits the lower
darkness as the twisted trees have several branches snapped and the vegetation
has been squashed flat in many places.
name: Oppressive Darkness

desc:
This narrow cavern is so deep that the towering cliffs on either side appear
almost to meet overhead. Though the black trees grow as thick as ever here,
there is a definite clear path forced between them, across which the grasping
branches do not pass. The ground underfoot is thoroughly tamped down, such that
Mirkwood's usual tangle of roots and brush has given way to mud.
name: Deep, Mucky Chasm

desc:
No natural light reaches the floor of this black chasm, as the walls to either
side close in ever tighter until they join up completely, creating an
underground passage to the east. The ground is churned into mud and even the
ever-present trees of Mirkwood have been beaten back to form a sizeable
clearing. A rough stake has been forced into the ground by the cave entrance,
and a strong smell of animal decay emanates from within the lair.
name: Outside the Lair

desc:
This black lair exudes an almost overpowering stench of decaying flesh. Upon the
rocks of the floor are semi-dried gobs of raw meat and entrails, along with a
number of crushed skulls and other shattered bones. On one wall, fanciful lines
and swirls are crudely drawn, and the dye of choice seems to be fresh blood.
Another wall seems to have been plastered together using blood, hair, fur, bone
and other grisly materials.
name: Hideously Decorated Lair

desc:
This deep fissure turns dramatically here. To the south, a stiff scramble uphill
leads to less dark but more heavily forested terrain, whereas to the west, the
cliffs close in completely overhead, forming a black cavern. The ground has been
churned up here and the trees have been beaten back somewhat to create an open
area at the mouth of the cave. From within the darkness, a strong stench of
animal decay billows.
name: Mouth of a Foul Cavern

desc:
The shadowed land drops suddenly here, sinking rapidly into a dark, narrow
fissure, with sharp cliffs rising up on each side. Branches have been broken
from the tortured trees, and the ground underfoot is compressed in places,
suggesting that something of considerable size has passed this way. Westwards,
the grim forest opens up, reveaaling sickly trees that are larger and more
widely spaced.
name: Dark, Treacherously Sloping Ground

desc:
Unlike much of Mirkwood, a tree stands alone here. But it is a behemoth of a
tree, unlike any other. Half rotten, yet large and strong, it appears to have
choked out every plant near it, leaving bare forest surrounding its gigantic,
gnarled roots. A thick black mist around the tree emits an awful odour.
name: Grotesque Behemoth

desc:
With the ground dropping away into darkness on both the east and west sides,
this area has the feeling of a bridge across a forbidding, black chasm below.
The trees are clearly not affected by vertigo as they pack as tightly as always
to every scrap of ground. The roots here are particularly treacherous, seeming
to curl out unexpectedly, attempting to pitch any passers-by over a precipice
and to their doom in the darkness far below.
name: Between Two Precipitous Drops

desc:
To the north and west, the trees suddenly stop as the ground drops vertically
away into a deep, black chasm. Faint sounds of movement and a hideous stench of
decaying plants and animals drift up from below. To the south, passage is also
blocked, this time by a tangled thicket of vines, laden with an evil sickness
that renders them putrid and slimy, even while they still grow. The only easy
way out of this stinking dead end is to the east where the foliage seems to open
up considerably.
name: Overlook into Darkness

desc:
Although the trees in this part of Mirkwood are not as tangled or closely set as
in many other areas, the ground vegetation has taken complete control of the
terrain instead. Deformed black nettles have overrun every surface, their
strangely twisted stems and leaves bristling with stiff hairs, ready to apply a
nasty sting at the slightest contact. Though movement through here is possible,
it is highly unlikely that any creatures of the forest would relish such an
experience.
name: Black Nettles

desc:
The trees in this wet and muddy area of the forest are smothered and mutilated
by thick, strangling vines. Forming a dense web, especially to the north where
passage is wholly blocked, these constricting growths appear laden with a
rotting sickness that infects all that they cover. This renders the entire area
putrid, the slimy vegetable matter decomposing even while it lives.
name: Web of Tendrils

desc:
The rocky path narrows here as coiled roots intrude from the south. Many of the
roots have been chopped off, and some still ooze a dark, distasteful fluid. A
rancid smell lurks about the area, and a thick, moist haze envelops the trail,
making breathing somewhat difficult. A crude, low arch is barely visible through
the mist to the east, while the trail to the west vanishes into the obscurity of
Mirkwood.
name: The Trail of Shadows

desc:
Crushed rock has been laid upon the dark, foul-smelling soil of the trail to Dol
Guldur, which runs east-west through the morose haze. Still, the ground is
treacherous; the trail slopes up to the east, and sharp rocks and pieces of
splintered bone await the chance to snag the unwary. Wicked, twisted trees close
in from all sides like a pack of deranged wolves, thirsty for warm blood.
name: The Trail of Shadows

desc:
The trail widens a bit here as it continues to travel under the limbs of the
overhanging trees. The trees are beginning to look more and more twisted by an
evil force the further east, this trail goes. To the east the trail begins to
turn into a hard packed road.
name: The Trail of Shadows

desc:
Thick, coiled roots strive against each other as they attempt to mine the dark
soil. The forest floor is so littered with them that only a couple of withering
shrubs grow here. All the other plants seem to have been choked away one by one.
To the south and east, fallen trees lie rotting after being uprooted by the
ruinous, malignant roots.
name: Grasping Roots

desc:
Covered in grisly carvings, a forbidding archway hangs low over the trail, which
continues to the west and east. The arch is vulgar and misshapen, and clearly
the work of some crude humanoid. Tree branches crowd close, obscuring some of
the detail, but one thing is clear: east through the gates and up into the
gloomy crags lies the malevolent citadel of Dol Guldur, overseen by a foul,
twisted tower.
name: The Trail of Shadows

desc:
An extraordinarily terrible infestation has taken over the surrounding oaks,
strangling them into a disfigured state. Entire trees crawl with millions of
voracious insects. A vicious fungal infection also spreads across each massive
root. The fungi quiver and convulse as they suck the life out of the massive
hardwoods.
name: Infested, Disfigured Oaks

desc:
The diseased forest floor is broken up only by the occasional putrid green
bubbling pool. Steam rises quickly from the simmering muck, and an
overwhelmingly rancid stench floods the thick air with each pop of a new bubble
surfacing. Though many pools are easily spotted, several others are barely
visible, camouflaged amidst the dense and invasive surroundings.
name: Coagulated Pools

desc:
Covered in rocks and pebbles, the misty road dips here as it runs east and west.
Visibility is limited, but a distant red glow can be seen high in the sky to the
east. A wide arch, barely visible in the dense gloom, crosses the trail to the
west. Sick, sinister trees push onto the trail from the south, threatening to
overtake the narrow road. There is a small cliff on the north side of the road,
with ill-tempered trees looming overhead.
name: The Trail of Shadows

desc:
This area of Mirkwood rises above its surroundings. The height does nothing to
dispel the darkness that claims the forest floor and nothing of the sky is
visible above. The forest thins to the south making it barely possible to see
some sort of trail or road.
name: Under the Eaves of Mirkwood

desc:
The road rises and falls at the whim of the forest, though the invasive foliage
is ever present. Twisted and malformed trees reach down from above with their
clawing branches, seemingly waiting to pounce on the unsuspecting. Dominating
the sky to the east, Dol Guldur sits encased in shadow, malevolent and brooding.
A smaller trail leaves the road, disappearing under the shadows of the trees to
the west.
name: Dark Juncture

desc:
The road continues to the north and south, lined with fallen trees and thick
brush, while a massive wall of dark foliage barricades the way to the west.
Pinned to the occasional tree is a gruesome, rotting, dead body, fully adorned
with a barely recognisable frozen face of horror and a few black carrion birds
pecking away at the rotting flesh.
name: The Road to Dol Guldur

desc:
The road narrows here, invaded by the surrounding trees and cluttered by thick
undergrowth. Various types of mould and lichen cover the forest floor,
discouraging anyone from straying from the path. A few of the trees look as
though they have been battered and beaten; their wounds seeping with a thick
black sap.
name: The Road to Dol Guldur

desc:
Void of leaves, the mix of black oaks and birches has been corrupted by the evil
that saturates the air. Two extremely large, black trees stand on both sides of
the road as ominous sentinels. Deep groans and the sound of slow, deliberate
movements seem to indicate that the forest itself is more alive than it would
appear.
name: The Road to Dol Guldur

desc:
To the west, the road dips into the foul oppressive gloom of the dark forest. To
the north, it winds up the hills of Dol Guldur. While the air of hills is less
foul than the woods to the west, it almost seems more foreboding. The hills to
east and south climb steeply. Sharp, jagged rocks cover the terrain that way,
making any passage impossible.
name: The Road to Dol Guldur

desc:
The thick forested surroundings are as abrasive and menacing as ever. Opaque
shadows dance between the trees, discernible at one moment and gone the next.
The black treacherous ground seems to constantly find a way to make the journey
through this area as troublesome as possible.
name: Forested Flank

desc:
Though the malice-filled forest is as sinister as ever to the north and west,
southwards a cliff drops down to a dark road. The ground here is very soft in
comparison to the surroundings, seemingly made up of rotten wood, branches,
moss, and pieces of decaying forest. Each step squishes into the decay with a
moist, bubbly gurgle.
name: Dense Trees above a Cliff

desc:
Vines and growths dangle from the low canopy of trees and emit a chokingly thick
smell of rot. The vines themselves drip with a thick black substance, seeping
and festering with the poison of Mirkwood. The rocky ground is covered in the
black substance, making the ground slippery and treacherous.
name: Festering Tangles

desc:
This part of the forest is filled with sounds of constant scraping and hissing
from the branches above, mixed with an occasional more substantial cracking.
Leaves and twigs spiral down, with a constant threat of something much weightier
crashing to the ground without warning. It is hard to tell whether the sound is
from some sort of vile animal, or from the trees themselves, but the malice is
unmistakable.
name: Under the Malignant Canopy

desc:
The ground rises up here to a small peak, but one so thickly forested with a
stand of dense trees and shrubs that it is impossible to see far in most
directions. Across the valley, a few miles to the southeast, the tall silhouette
of Dol Guldur rises up, craggy and menacing. A massive dark fortress sits atop
it, towering into the black mists that swirl around the naked hill's summit.
name: Dark Stand above a Cliff

desc:
The ground is unusually open here, with trunks of the surrounding trees spaced
wide apart almost as if purposefully planted in a circle many ages ago. On the
ground are strewn several rounded boulders, most of which are splattered with a
great deal of blood, dried into a gruesome black crust. Some intense violence
has happened here repeatedly. To the north and west, the open ground has been
overrun by intense thorn-laden undergrowth, and passage to the east is hindered
by a large pile of burnt rubble.
name: Massive, Circular Grove

desc:
Enormous, wide-trunked yews grow here, taller and more ancient than in many
other areas of Mirkwood. Indeed, it appears that these corrupt yews may have
purposefully been planted in a large circle in ages gone by, based on the round
clearing that extends west of here. The malice of the forest is as palpable as
ever with the sinister, dark trees looming threateningly overhead.
name: Massive, Sinister Trunks

desc:
The forest floor is covered with rotting, ancient detritus, save a stamped
circular area inside a thicket of dark oaks. Bleached bones and half-eaten
corpses lie scattered around, some of them almost buried under the foetid
leaves.
name: Deep, Decaying Detritus

desc:
A few gaps in the canopy provide a rare glimpse into the sky above. To the
north, a sheer cliff drops away into a black gorge, where the tops of the
twisted trees can be seen some distance below. On the opposite side, a similar
cliff rises up, beyond which stretches the seemingly endless, unyielding forest
of Mirkwood. Scattered along the ground is a large, wet pile, the stinking
contents of which are not immediately recognisable.
name: Sharp Drop

desc:
There is a stale, breathless silence here, broken only by constant dripping from
the moisture-laden mosses and lichens that cover every surface, down to the
forest floor. The very trees appear to be keeping still, as if the entire area
is holding its breath. The thick mat of decaying mosses underfoot and the sticky
webs of silk overhead muffle sound so thoroughly that this would be an ideal
place for the predators of Mirkwood to hunt, which the countless bones of
unfortunate animals give testament to.
name: Hunting Grounds

desc:
Despite the relatively open ground here, a deep and forbidding mist permeates
the dark terrain. Occasional threads of silk hang from the canopy above, and the
ground is littered with half-rotten sticks, loose stones, and the occasional
bone, making quiet travel a challenge for all but the smallest and most nimble
of creatures.
name: Misty Forest

desc:
The air is thick here, full of nasty spores and dust. Great tendrils of dark
moss and lichen hang low from the branches of each tree. Some of the tendrils
are rotting but most are long-dead and surprisingly bone dry. They constantly
add to the vile motes floating in the air as they move even in the slight,
haze-shrouded breeze. The resulting atmosphere is painful to inhale, cloying
one's tongue and throat with the foul taste of ancient death.
name: Rotting, Dusty Lichen

desc:
A thick, dark haze hangs about the menacing trees and extends in all directions.
The darkness is as oppressive as ever, bearing down its evil weight on all who
venture here. A minuscule amount of breathable air seems to sneak through the
tangles of the amazingly dense and twisted undergrowth. Eastwards, the ground
drops away into a small brush-filled gully, making travel around it far easier
than through it.
name: Foreboding, Stone-Covered Woods

desc:
The trees here begin to grow very tightly together, causing a natural dead end.
The trunks of the trees are so thick here that the there is no way to get
through. There is a dark gloom that hovers over this area as the trees block all
light and capture a musty mist which hangs over the forest.
name: Thickening Dead End

desc:
Twisted and mutilated branches criss-cross between the trees so thickly that
there is scarcely any room to wriggle between them. The cruel boughs, often
sporting rancid fungal growths or wicked thorns, seem almost consciously aware
as they manage to always be positioned right where they can gash unprotected
skin. A dark void opens up in the forest floor to the south, with cliffs
dropping down into blackness.
name: Clawing Branches

desc:
There is nowhere to turn here, save west. To the north and east, brambles of
such ferocity bar the way, evil thorns protruding threateningly in every
direction, that to cut through would take days. To the south, a dizzying void
opens up, with slick cliffs dropping into utter darkness. Even overhead, the
mass of groping branches forms a roof as impenetrable as any formed by hand.
Sticks and roots protrude from the ground, along with some old bones. A couple
of discoloured skulls stare vacantly from underneath the brambles.
name: Dead End

desc:
The evil, black forest pushes relentlessly in on all sides, but especially from
under foot. The ground is a massive tangle of twisted, tortured roots that seem
almost sentient in their ability to catch and hold anyone travelling through
them. The rotting stench of decay that rises up cannot be wholly explained by
plant matter alone. The vegetation is thickest to the north, making movement in
that direction impractical.
name: Root-Riddled Forest

desc:
Thickets bristling with cruel scimitar-shaped thorns abound in this area,
perhaps fed by a putrid, black pool that they cluster densely around. Even the
trees are surrounded and covered by these cruel growths, such that a path can
scarcely be forged through them in any direction without the risk of some
injury. Indeed, many of the sharp spikes are stained black where they have
punctured creatures that have previously passed this way.
name: Thorny Thickets

desc:
The ground is rough and uneven here, sloping downwards to the north into an area
of increasing gloom and palpable evil. With the thick undergrowth and
ever-present darkness, it is hard to see far in any direction, but noises from
the nearby gloom indicate the presence of denizens. Some of the rustling is
extremely close. Dense thickets of cruel thorns deny movement to the south from
here.
name: Cruel, Uneven Ground

desc:
The undergrowth in this part of the black forest is almost as thick as the
ever-present mesh of branches above. Spiny brambles with long, cruel thorns
catch and grasp at all but the most deft of creatures. Navigation through these
tangled briars is impossible to the north and scarcely less so to the south,
though the forest appears to open up a little elsewhere, especially eastwards
where the ground drops away to a steep, rocky slope.
name: Spiky, Tangled Brambles

desc:
This is one of the more precarious hillsides in Mirkwood. The black mass of
trees in every direction make it hard to make out the surrounding land. But it
is clear that there are sharp upward gradients in all directions save east,
where the ground descends into a deep, black chasm.
name: Black, Sloping Forest

desc:
Though it slopes slightly downhill to the east, the lie of the land is fairly
flat in this area of Mirkwood, but the foetid, evil gloom is as deep and dark as
ever. The aged, unyielding trees weave such a dense tangle of branches and
leaves up above that it is quite impossible to discern which limb belongs to
which tree. Passages to the north, south, and west are blocked equally by
impenetrable growth, leaving east the only way out of this oppressive forest.
name: Deep, Dank Gloom

desc:
The black, twisted trees of Mirkwood cluster together in all directions, save
north where a steep cliff descends into the deeper darkness of a festering
chasm. The ground is sparsely covered with thin, spiky brush and the occasional
clump of sickly ivy, with its tattered and discoloured leaves.
name: Overlooking a Dark Gorge

desc:
The unscalable cliff to the north drops off into a deep, murky chasm, and the
air is as thick and foetid as ever. Dark trees are thick with moss, their
branches twisted and black. Whispers from an unknown evil fade in and out with
the wind, giving a bleak, haunted undertone to the sounds of the forest.
name: Rocky Cliff

desc:
The ground slopes sharply downwards to the north, the darkness increasing
dramatically in that direction too. Travel is not made any easier by the
frequent, jagged boulders in the area, many covered in a stinking, slimy fungus
that attracts clouds of huge, black flies. The undergrowth rustles and sways
from time to time as something scurries through the dense, rotting plant cover,
just out of sight.
name: Slimy, Rotting Forest

desc:
Even at the bottom of this deep ravine, the dense trees hardly yield. Water
drips down from the cliffs above, forming a foul, murky pool among the rocks and
trees. A cruel haze hangs above the ground, obscuring the pond, as well as the
rest of the low-lying chasm. To the north and south are impassable, sheer
cliffs, but the wall to the west is a steep, slimy slope. The land is flattest
to the east, but the air gets rapidly heavier with darkness and decay in that
direction.
name: Deep, Dreary Chasm

desc:
Not only does the rotting and grasping growth of Mirkwood cluster as thick as
ever at the bottom of this ravine, but the cliff walls to the north and south
are lined with stunted, clawing trees. Their torturous roots bite deeply at the
living rock in a constant struggle to support their tenuous positions, an action
that slowly breaks the very rock apart.
name: Between Sombre, Towering Cliffs

desc:
This foul, black chasm is at its deepest and darkest point at this location.
Finally, even the twisted trees have left the sheer cliff faces alone here,
which yields to nothing save some patches of black, rotting moss. A large fault
scores diagonally across the southern cliff, and the dark layers of rock to one
side are clearly offset against the same strata on the other. A rotten stench
emanates from the fault as if the very bowels of the earth are adding their evil
effluence to Mirkwood.
name: Bottom of a Deep Chasm

desc:
The ground is particularly uneven here, even by the treacherous standards of
Mirkwood. Though the deep-rooted trees hold on with no trouble, the surface is a
constant tangle of loose rocks, ensnaring undergrowth, and hidden potholes. The
ground sinks rapidly into stagnant darkness to the west while slimy, laborious
slopes climb upward to the east and south. A massive, crumbling black cliff
blocks all passage to the north.
name: Treacherous Rise

desc:
Progress through this part of Mirkwood involves scrambling over large
slime-covered boulders and pushing through thick, rotting roots. The undergrowth
appears to intentionally hamper the traveller, with any headway north or south
entirely impossible. The aged trees, primarily dark-leaved oaks and twisted
beeches, grow large here, though they are covered in festering sap-warts.
name: Dark, Rocky Roots

desc:
The Road to Dol Guldur turns here, climbing up the dark, barren hills of black
basalt to the east, while winding toward the forest to the south. To the north,
a small, craggy trail leads off into the edge of the forest. Passage to the west
is impossible, as the slopes descend abruptly into the festering blackness of
Mirkwood.
name: Crossroads at the Foot of Dol Guldur

desc:
The road seems heavily travelled, worn with the beat of countless hooves and the
turns of heavy wagon wheels. The foreboding shadow of Dol Guldur rises to the
east, while the road approaches a vast forest to the west. To the south, the
land is a mix of hills and trenches. It seems passable, if not for a large pile
of broken rock blocking the land between the hills.
name: The Road to Dol Guldur

desc:
The rock-covered trail skirts alongside a deep and long east-west ravine.
Twisted trees, as solidly rooted in dark, corrupted soil as ever, crowd thickly
down the treacherous slope into the pitch black fissure below. The twisting
trail, though narrow here and to the north, appears to widen considerably
towards the west. The putrid stench of death and decay closes in on all sides,
especially from the fissure below.
name: Reeking, Narrow Trail

desc:
Twisting through the trees, this rock-strewn trail appears almost as tortured
and maimed as the very plants it seeks to pass by. The ground drops sharply on
the western side, descending into a small yet impenetrable gully of thorny
brush. Yet elsewhere, despite the ever-looming oppression of Mirkwood's evil,
travel is possible without immediate hindrance.
name: Dim, Meandering Track

desc:
Passing along the dividing line between malice-filled forest and looming rocky
hill, a palpable chill haunts this stretch of the trail. It picks a cautious
path between the doom-laden boughs of Mirkwood to the west, yet swings sharply
to the north to avoid the black, boulder-strewn treachery of the bare Dol Guldur
hills immediately east of here. A sickening stench of recent death wafts in from
the north.
name: Trail through the Tangled Trees

desc:
The foothills of the eerie bluffs of Dol Guldur rise up from the oppressive evil
of Mirkwood. The ground hits an almost sheer rock face to the east, yet tumbles
precariously downward on the south side into a deep and noxious ravine. The
smell of decay and death drifts up from below, while foggy, haunting mists
cascade down from the craggy mountainside above, like ancient whispers of
tormented souls just on the edge of hearing.
name: Steep, Forested Slope

desc:
The Road to Dol Guldur continues through the hills here, climbing towards the
summit. Black rocks line the road to the north and south, forming inhospitable
hills of dark basalt. To the east, the omnious shadow of Dol Guldur looms over
the horizon, filling the sky with malevolent energy.
name: The Road to Dol Guldur

desc:
The road through the forest turns here, heading deeper into the depths of the
wood. Sharp branches and spines line the edge of the road, ready to punish any
who stray from the path. Black roots have completely commandeered some areas,
while some sharp, broken rocks make any slip potentially deadly.
name: The Road to Dol Guldur

desc:
The road through the dark forest turns here, twisting around some massive,
gnarled trees and a brush-covered hill. The ground around the road is broken,
black earth, covered with oozing vines and slick, oily fauna.
name: The Road to Dol Guldur

desc:
The roots of the invasive black trees have erupted here, making the road a bit
bumpy. Large thorn bushes line the lane, equipped with nasty looking spines that
drip with some dark substance. The road continues to the east and west, just as
dark and gloomy as it is here.
name: The Road to Dol Guldur

desc:
The malformed trees, moist and black, encroach on the road from all sides. Mould
and disease dominate the foliage, constantly filling the air with a harsh, thick
atmosphere. The road appears to continue to the east and west, but the thick
trees make it hard to see.
name: The Road to Dol Guldur

desc:
A long, winding road passes through Mirkwood, its path meandering between the
enormous trunks of the ancient trees. The road is made of packed dirt with two
wide wagon ruts cut into the earth. An oppressive gloom besets the road, the
far-reaching branches of the trees form a ceiling above, shielding even the road
from the sky above. A large camp is visible to the north, protected by a tall
wooden gate, and a spiked palisade.
name: Road before a Large Camp

desc:
The trail winds through dense forest, the trees on either side so thick as to
make any passage impossible. To the north, a crude timber wall, running east to
west, rises up from the ground like an ugly scar on the landscape. The tip of
each timber has been sharpened into a vicious point, while the base of the wall
has been reinforced with deadly stakes.
name: Trail to a Camp

desc:
A worn-down trail winds its way in between two small hills. The knoll to the
west burns with an eerie light, bellowing a thick column of black smoke, and the
ash raining down has turned the trail and surrounding ground pitch-black. A huge
banner tops the small bluff to the east, showing a hideous black dragon as a
warning to invaders. To the south, the trail connects to a dark road winding
through the forest, while northwards it meets an ominous gate leading into a
filthy camp.
name: Blackened Trail near a Camp

desc:
Surrounded on the west and south by dense forest flatland, this knoll looks out
of place. Closer inspection of the soggy ground reveals that it is in fact not
made of earth, but of a mixture of ash, bones, gravel, and plain junk. There is
a large pile of rotting flesh, trash and refuse that is apparently fuel for a
permanently burning bonfire. The fire is massive, spewing forth suffocating
smoke and ash like a small volcano.
name: Burning Knoll

desc:
Suffocating black clouds of smoke weave around a broad, grim gate, consisting of
two spiked metal wings. The two halves are black metal, held up by two enormous
twisted stone pillars on either side. A crude but ghastly image of a dragon has
been painted in blood on the front of the gate.
name: Trail before a Bloody Gate

desc:
A small knoll rises up in the forested hills here. In the centre of the knoll, a
large cut timber has been fixed into the ground. A black banner with a red
painted dragon flies from the timber. The hills rise up in most directions,
making passage impossible any way but to the west.
name: Small Knoll

desc:
The forest of Mirkwood climbs up into the hills here. A few spots of bare, black
rock peak through the hillside amidst the dense foliage. Elsewhere, the dense
undergrowth of Mirkwood is as thick as ever. To the west, the hills drop sharply
down into the forest.
name: Dark, Forested Hill

desc:
This forest might have once been green and healthy, but terrific storms have
uprooted many trees and thrown them around like small twigs. Between the slowly
rotting trunks and stumps, new trees have grown until new storms uprooted them
too. Hundreds of years and hundreds of storms later this area is a chaotic
labyrinth of undergrowth, trees and dead wood.
name: Maze of Fallen Trees

desc:
A hole in the undergrowth has been carved out here, creating a well-hidden
escape from the malicious trees and poisonous spines. Strewn about are the bones
and bone fragments of various creatures, possibly the prey of something that
lives here. To the north, the backside of the hole is blocked by a large black
boulder jutting up from the earth. Some carvings can be seen along its edge.
name: Bone-littered Hideaway

desc:
This forest is ancient, dark, and brooding. The thick crowns of the old trees
hold the light at bay. Under the canopy, the forest floor is home to a large
variety of shrubs and vines that thrive in the shadows. The branches of the
trees are clad in drapes of poisonous-looking fungi and tendril-like vines.
name: Dark Forest

desc:
A very narrow ledge among the darkly forested hills allows access to a wooded
area to the west. The rolling slopes throw their shadows like massive cloaks
over the surrounding woodland. Far to the north-east, a giant spire of black
rock juts out of the ground like the blade of a black knife.
name: Narrow Ledge

desc:
These hills begin to mark the outskirts of Dol Guldur, they are made of the same
black basalt as the surrounding hills. The hills to the north and west are
higher and appear even more black and shrouded in shadows. These hills are
treeless but not barren, full of cracks and crevices, but still they support
some hardy shrubs. A dark forest grows at the foot of the hills to the south.
name: Lower Part of the Black Hills

desc:
Black hills rise up in the north, but a larger, cone-shaped hill to the south
stretches even higher. There seems to be a path winding up the hill. Along the
path, a little hollow gives some shelter. The remains of an old campsite show
that others have taken refuge here.
name: Lower Part of the Black Hills

desc:
A wide canyon to the west separates this part of the land from a high cliff
overlooking the lower and forested hills to the south. This area appears less
cracked and eroded than the hills to the north and east, but it is still a
treacherous maze of rock formations and steep-walled, debris littered canyons.
name: East of a Cliff

desc:
The hills around are dark, bare, and very steep, the slopes to the north and
east all but impassable. Far to the north-east a tall rockspire rises above the
surrounding hills. A small cleft leads down into a narrow valley covered with
loose gravel and larger rocks.
name: Black Hills

desc:
Like all of the hills around Dol Guldur, these are also bare. A few thin weeds
struggling to find nourishment, but nothing else covers the land. To the north,
a huge spire of rock towers above, dominating the landscape. The craggy walls of
the spire are rough, but appear impossible to climb.
name: Black Hills

desc:
The hills are made of bare and uneven ground. Steep slopes full of cracks and
fissures make the travelling unsafe. No sign of wildlife can be seen around. All
is covered by dust and even the poorest weed will not grow here. Far to the
north a strange rocky structure raises from the hills.
name: Black Hills

desc:
These hills are the foot of the black hills which raise sharply to the north and
east. The hills are steep and sharp-edged, their shape formed by erosion which
leads to many cracks and a ground littered with rocks and stone. The whole area
lacks plantlife, as there is no soil left for plants to grow.
name: Lower Part of the Black Hills

desc:
These rough, jagged hills are desolate and devoid of vegetation. The stones form
bizarre formations of black and grey spear-like spires, rising in groups of tens
or dozens. Some are just a few feet high, while others eclipse the tallest
trees. They stand in groups, circles, and other patterns that look almost
unnatural.
name: Black Hills

desc:
These wooded hills butt up against a taller plateau to the north and east. A
thick layer of fertile soil covers what seems to be basalt underneath. In some
places bizarre pillars and formations of black rock jut up through the soil and
undergrowth. Thick roots snake across the ground, poking up through the forest
floor, even so the trees seem to flourish, their dense crowns forming a green
ceiling overhead.
name: Shadowy Hills

desc:
These low, wooded hills mark the southern border between Mirkwood and Dol
Guldur. Little can be seen through the dense forest of the rising hills to the
north, yet the upward slope is undeniable. The slope continues downhill to the
west, into a shadowy dell. Most of the trees here are firs, with some birches
and hoary old oaks in small pockets.
name: Low, Wooded Hill

desc:
Nestled between inhospitable black hills is a large dell with steep hillsides to
the north and south. Its bottom is densely wooded and the muddy ground is
shrouded in everlasting shadow and darkness. There are many rocks and boulders
at the foot of the slope, washed down by erosion and landslides.
name: Dark Forest Dell

desc:
It seems the evil in hills of Dol Guldur has influenced the trees that grow here
at their feet. The trees are grown like old willows with long branches hanging
down to the ground, but at a closer look their leaves have more similarity to
oaks and their fruit look like fir-cones. The trees seem to move in the darkness
and stalk any trespassers.
name: Strange Trees

desc:
Hills cover this landscape , and their size grow towards the west. Every rise,
valley, and hilltop is covered with dark, brooding trees, most of them fir or
birch. The hills here are dwarfed by a plateau, a higher part of Dol Guldur,
which towers to the north and northwest.
name: Dark and Sloping Forest

desc:
Forest covers these slopes, conspiring with the plateau to the north to keep
these woods in permanent shadows. The slopes ascend gently towards the west,
slowly climbing towards higher heights. The trees are gnarled and ancient, some
of them true behemoths that must have been standing here since the the time of
the last alliance.
name: Craggy Slope beneath a Plateau

desc:
This brush-covered area is squeezed between dark woods to the north and a steep
plateau to the south. The ground is very stony, but the poor soil seems adequate
for a variety of plantlife consisting of brown, tough shrubs, wild grass, as
well as some larger thornbushes.
name: Brush in the Shadow of a Plateau

desc:
This patch of brushland is squeezed between the forest to the north and a
plateau to the south. The plantlife consists mainly of low, brown shrubs and
long, thorny vines that creep across the ground for lack of trees to climb. The
plateau to the south overshadows these lower lands, casting them into gloom for
much of the day.
name: Brush between Mirkwood and a Plateau

desc:
This area has been clear-cut in the past. Old, rotten stumps can be seen dotting
the landscape. No one cleared the underbrush, which, liberated from the
suffocating ceiling of leaves, has grown to an immense height and density. The
brush is bordered by dense, shadowy forest to the north and south.
name: Deforested Brush

desc:
The ground of this dark forest is wet and muddy, resembling more the surface of
a swamp than a forest floor. The smell of rot is everywhere and the air trapped
under the cover of the dark crowns is barely breathable. A steep cliff to the
south is slowly eroding away, the ground beneath littered with small crumbling
rocks.
name: Rotting Forest

desc:
The forest canopy closes in here, leaving the dark shadow of a moonless night or
a tunnel deep under the mountains. The trees are bend and crooked, and their
roots seem to grown together. There is no undergrowth except of a few rings of
fungi, which look poisonous upon closer look. The almost vertical cliffs of dark
hills form the southern border of the forest which continues east.
name: Black and Lightless Forest

desc:
The hills narrow here between a high wall of black rock to the north and a flat
plateau to the southeast. The northern wall forms the buttress of a strange
spire of rock which stretches straight up out of the ground. To the east, the
hills give way to dark, twisted forest.
name: Hilly Passage

desc:
This is without a doubt the highest peak southeast of Dol Guldur. Shaped like a
perfect cone, it towers over the Forest of Mirkwood, with slopes that are steep
and brittle. The black mountain consists of a very soft, grainy stone and some
harder, glassy stones which are strewn over the flanks. An excellent view is
afforded over the whole surrounding area.
name: On a Black Hilltop

desc:
Blood and gore is spattered everywhere, both on the trail itself and across the
surrounding plant-growth. A terrible fight has occurred here recently, though
the nature of the loser cannot be determined. Globs of flesh, covered in flies,
semi-dried pools of blood, and some fragments of bone litter the rocky path, yet
there is no sign of a carcass.
name: Blood-Soaked Trail

desc:
Ascending above the treetops of Mirkwood, the usual twisted tangle of dark
vegetation gives way reluctantly to more open lands, though the fractured black
rock and cinder that is strewn across the landscape lends itself no more to
travelling than the dim forest of Mirkwood. Sharp, ruthless inclines to the east
and south block further progress upwards, and torturous scree-descents to the
north and west are the only ways down.
name: Rock-Strewn Hillside

desc:
There is blood on this trail. Dark streaks are smeared all across the ground,
and the undergrowth has been pushed down roughly. Whatever caused the gore was
clearly not hindered by the thick, thorny brambles in the least. The faint path
heads northwards into the gloom, and, along with the smears, to the west. A
rough scree slope climbs sharply upwards a little to the south, leading into the
grim, haze-shrouded hills of Dol Guldur.
name: Bloody Trail

desc:
The land is slowly rising in the south, where the grey hills of Dol Guldur are.
The soil of this slope does not support larger trees, but gives enough
nourishment for the many nettle plants that grow between the sharp-edged
boulders and rocks.
name: Slope of Nettles

desc:
Crude looking hills with many small cliffs, cracks, crevices and stone covered
slopes mark this land. The whole area looks like it was carved by a crazed
giant. The trees are sparse and provide no shadows, but the hills are not
entirely lacking in vegetation. In some soil filled cracks thorny bushes and
stinging nettles have stubbornly taken root.
name: Treeless Hills

desc:
This plateau in the hills is covered by piles and piles of slag and debris,
likely the remnants of an old mine. A long time seems to have passed since it
was last used. Between the rocks, signs of life are bursting forth in the form
of saplings and small shrubs. But the mining waste gives almost no nourishment
so the plants are thin and sickly, though still thorny.
name: Piles of Slag

desc:
Dense vegetation covers the hills of this area, but the plants are brown and
look sickly. Most of them are rooted in small cracks on the stony ground. There
are tiny tunnels through the thick scrub and small pieces of fur collected on
occasional longer thorns. To the south the hills become more stony, more cracked
and even more inhospitable.
name: Hill covered with Thorny Shrubs

desc:
Obviously someone mined for something here a long time ago. There are several
old holes in the ground, although most of them crumbled a long time ago. Of the
heavy wooden frames that stand guard over them, only rotten, ivy-covered
remnants can be seen. One dark opening, a black maw in the hillside, has
survived the ravages of time better than most.
name: Above an Abandoned Mine

desc:
The grey, almost black, hills of basalt are covered with ash. Small veins are
visible in the rock, showing up in the red colour of rust that indicates a high
degree of iron. Between the hills to the west there are ruined clusters of
ancient buildings which look like two old and long abandoned mines.
name: Hills Overlooking Two Old Mines

desc:
The vast shadow of Dol Guldur's mighty fortress looms ever present over these
hills. The stronghold stands ever resolute, ignoring the ravages of time. Its
malevolent presence emits an aura of brutal enmity, buffeted by the dark,
twisted power of nearby Mirkwood. A stone wall, black as coal, rises up from the
hills to the west to form the massive foundation of the baleful citadel.
name: In the Shadow of the Fortress

desc:
The mine to the north has produced massive quantities of waste. These hills are
largely a result of heavy mining for many years. Climbing the hills is difficult
because they are made of the loose slag from the mine. Each step makes the small
rocks slide underfoot, which gives the impression that a wrong step could start
a large slide. Two massive, withered, trees grow here, with a messy pile of
branches between them.
name: Slag Hills

desc:
A few ruined stone buildings are clustered here, though their long-destroyed
walls are razed to rubble, and there are no remnants of roofs or doors. Beside
them, a deep pit has been haphazardly dug into the rust-tinged rock, though the
mine workings have long since been abandoned. Multiple rockfalls have cascaded
into the dark, open quarry, providing many hiding places where some predators
could be lurking.
name: Old Quarry

desc:
This clearing ascends slowly into the southern hills of Dol Guldur. The whole
clearing is covered with patches of stinging nettles and bushes with sharp
thorns. A steep, crumbling cliff blocks travel to the east while the dark forest
of Mirkwood extends gloomily in the north and west.
name: Thornbushes

desc:
The forest is thick and dark all around here. The trees look old and strong, but
are strangely twisted and bent. The smell of rotten leaves lingers in the
stagnant air. The forest continues to the east and west, while to the south a
clearing is visible.
name: Dark Forest

desc:
Black trees cover the area. Many of them are dead. The air is stagnant and
smells like the whole forest is slowly rotting away. Dead plants and thorny
vines cover the ground. It seems it is only a matter of years until this area is
as barren and treeless as the hills to the south and east.
name: Under Dark Trees

desc:
This stretch of forest lies in the shadows of Dol Guldur that looms to the
south. The trees look sickly, their roots covered with fungi. The smell of
rotten plants stands in the air. A small pond with black, muddy water lies close
to the sheer cliff that marks the beginning of the mountainous land to the
south. The expanse of Mirkwood continues to the east and west, following the
rise of the hills.
name: Smelly Pool in the Forest

desc:
Black trees, most of them dark oaks, cover the slopes of this gently hilly area.
There are heavily forested slopes in all directions except south, where the
terrible hills of Dol Guldur hold the border of Mirkwood at bay. One high hill
holds one single very large tree, it is twisted as though something has caused
it great pain.
name: At a Twisted Tree

desc:
The land ascends towards the east and the south. To the west are smaller hills,
all thickly forested. The forest continues here, but the closer it is to the
bald hills, the more trees appear to have withered and died, as though there is
something poisonous that prevents them from taking root. Those twisted trees
have thin, leafless crowns, and many have fallen.
name: Deforested Edge of Mirkwood

desc:
The land rises up from the lowlands to the west, and continue ever upwards to
the east. There are some trees here, and a lot of undergrowth. Some
deforestation has taken place towards the west near the foot of the hills.
name: Green Hills

desc:
The terrain is difficult to travel. The hills are steep and criss-crossed by
small cracks and fissures. A dense forest grows on the slopes, with plenty of
undergrowth, mostly thornbushes, that make movement difficult. Suddenly the
forest ends, as the hill ends in almost vertical drops to the east and south.
name: Wooded Peak

desc:
The slopes of these cracked hills are densely covered by a variety of plantlife.
Most have either nasty thorns or look poisonous and sickly, with leaves covered
in thin slimy layers of vicious fluids. The slopes are caught between steeper
hills to the west and Mirkwood to the east. A jagged crevice splits the hillside
apart to the south, too wide to jump or otherwise pass safely.
name: Cracked, Broken Hills

desc:
Dark pines dominate this valley, but a strong storm has uprooted many of the old
trees. A bright, green moss covers the fallen trees. A thick, brown layer of
pine needles covers the ground, giving a soft crackle whenever stepped on. To
the south, the forest thickens even more, and the shadows reign under the trees.
name: Adumbral Vale

desc:
The forest consists of dark oaks and groves of tall, black firs. The ground is
soft. Thick layers of rotting compost fill the air with their smell. Entangling
roots and vines are hidden under the surface, forcing every traveller to be
cautious. Despite the rich plantlife, the forest lacks any sign of animal life.
name: Adumbral Wildwood

desc:
This forest is extremely dark and the trees look very old. Most are gnarly oaks
with dark, almost black, bark. The trees rustle, crack, and creak constantly, as
if agitated. There is little undergrowth visible, as scant sunlight reaches the
ground. In the northern part of this area grow many bushes of somehow strange
roses. Their long needle-like thorns make travel perilous.
name: Adumbral Woodland

desc:
The woods here are made up of ancient trees, covered with moss and creeping
vines that draw their nourishment from the vast trunks. The ground below the
trees lies in an eternal shadow. The undergrowth is very sparse except on the
southern border where the forest thins into a clearing.
name: Adumbral Forest Floor

desc:
Black oaks and alders make up this part of the forest. Their limbs and leaves
form a dense ceiling that blocks out all light. The ground is soft and muddy and
the air is stagnant. Sickly grey-green lichen and patches of fungi cover many of
the trunks.
name: Adumbral Mirkwood

desc:
Once this area was a part of Mirkwood, but in recent years it has been
deforested. The removal of the trees seems to have given room for a wide variety
of thorny bushes to thrive. A gigantic rockspire rises to the west, blocking the
view of the rest of Dol Guldur.
name: Deforested Brush

desc:
The forest is dense and dark, the leaves of the old, black-barked oaks form a
ceiling that allows not a single sunbeam to reach the ground. Great tangles of
vines embrace the towering trunks, disappearing into the canopy. To the west and
the south thick underbrush marks the borders of the forest.
name: Dark Woods

desc:
The forest is dark and silent. The trees are hoary old oaks, their bark rough
and almost black. The undergrowth consists of thorny rose bushes that seem to
strangle the trees. The flowers on the bushes are mottled in shades of grey and
even though they look like they should be rotting, they are in full bloom.
name: Dark Woods

desc:
These low hills border Mirkwood to the east and south. Though the trees have
found no purchase here, there are some smaller plants. A deep crevice separates
these slopes from those to the north. Several large boulders dot the slopes,
some as big as houses; apparently the debris from landslides off the higher hill
to the west.
name: Between the Hills and the Forest

desc:
A dead-end valley lie nestled between stony slopes to the north, south, and
west. The valley's floor is densely forested by great, gnarly oaks clad in
drapes of thorny vines. The forest continues to the east, equally dark and
ominous.
name: Dark, Forested Valley

desc:
This hillside slopes away from the tall rockspike to the south. At the bottom of
a steep drop to the west lies a canyon from which a fearsome stench rises. But
the most remarkable sight here is the enormous pile of rubble at the foot of the
rockspire. It looks like like hundreds and hundreds of troll statues, all broken
into rubble from a great fall.
name: Among Troll-shaped Rubble

desc:
Here, in the depth of Mirkwood, the land is shrouded in shadows and mirk by the
thick layers of branch and leaf overhead. The air is humid and stagnant with the
smells of rot and decay. Thick layers of fallen leaves, knee-deep in some
places, cover the forest floor.
name: Under Adumbral Canopies

desc:
These hills are crumbling away like rust from ancient iron. To the west the
cliff edge is still solid and high, but in the east nothing appears to hold a
natural shape. Rocks are strewn around as though thrown by mythical giants. In a
few of the many cracks hardy plants have rooted themselves. Most of the plants
protect themselves with long thorns against the few beings that travel this
inhospitable land.
name: Rugged Hills

desc:
Grey walls of rock form a small and twisting canyon. The canyon floor is
littered with boulders, rocks and stones, it seems the walls are slowly eroding
away leaving the canyon wider at the bottom than overhead. Torn clothes, smashed
pottery, dented and dulled metal lie everywhere, mixed with bleaching bones. A
few remains of beings and their belongings that dropped into the canyon from
above are spiked on rockspires. An odour of rotting flesh fills the canyon.
name: Garbage-strewn Canyon

desc:
The walls of this canyon lean overhead and consist of brittle, cracking rock.
Impossible to climb they allow travel only to the north and south. The canyon
floor is littered with debris from the eroding canyon walls and rubbish. Smashed
and broken bones, rotting clothes, pieces of leather and broken weapons cover
the ground. The air is stagnant and there are tones of rotting flesh. A small
arch of rock spans high over the canyon like a natural bridge.
name: Deep Canyon

desc:
The walls of this canyon are steep and hang over, making it impossible to climb
them. The canyon floor is littered with small rocks and refuse of every type The
distinctive odour of excrement covers every surface. About halfway up the canyon
a tunnel has been carved out of the rock but the steep walls prevent any access.
It is evidently the source of this plethora of junk and rubbish.
name: Deep Canyon

desc:
This rocky canyon slopes up to the north and down to the south, while thick rock
walls block passage to the east and west. The canyon is filled with eroded dirt
and rubble which makes travel tricky and possibly dangerous to those who fail to
take care. A strange looking rock-spire looms above to the southeast.
name: Sloping Canyon Floor

desc:
This section of the canyon is very narrow, the walls lean overhead and are
spanned by a gated stone block bridge. It seems some lazy being throws waste
directly off the bridge without thought or worry for the canyon below. There are
broken glass vials, pots and even the occasional bunch of giant bones littering
the ground. The western canyon wall looks slightly unnatural, on a closer looks
slightly smoother than the rest of the canyon walls.
name: Under a Stone Bridge

desc:
The canyon widens towards the south, but ends there at a vertical wall of rock.
The canyon walls are too eroded to climb out. Every wall and facet of this
canyon shouts 'dead end'. Scattered bones show the presence of carnivorous
beings, though there appear to be no tracks and no evidence of struggles. The
mouth of the canyon is wide and it would be nearly certain death to fall into
such a place.
name: Dead-end

desc:
A dark tunnel runs north into darkness and east out into the canyon. The walls
are rough-cut rock that must have been hollowed out ages ago to make the way
somewhat passable. It is evident that the passage has not been used in some
time, as a thin layer of wet grime covers every surface.
name: Tunnel Entrance

desc:
One wall of the tunnel has collapsed inward, leaving rubble and rock strewn
across the ground. Much of the stone has been hewn by miners searching for the
ore within. A few deep furrows run to the west, possibly made by carts hauling
ore away in years long past. A small, opening leads into the darkness to the
east.
name: Collapsed Tunnel

desc:
A narrow path has been carved into the black basalt rock. The tunnel connects to
the outside world to the west, and the way up is very steep. To the east the
passage levels out where it leads into a wider area. Several rough tracks in the
rocky floor of the tunnel suggests that heavy things were hauled through here in
the past.
name: Old Mineshaft

desc:
No light penetrates this section of the tunnel. The walls are made of dark rock
mixed with thick, black earth. The floor is a mix of sure footing and soft mud
that can trip up even the most experienced travellers. To the east, the rock
gives way to earth and clay as the passage narrows.
name: Black Tunnel

desc:
An old mineshaft extends to the west from this tunnel. The basalt walls are
pocked from old mining yet the floor of the cavern is surprisingly smooth and
even, as if this tunnel has seen a lot more traversing than a dead-end mineshaft
would merit.
name: Dirty Tunnel

desc:
This narrow tunnel seems about ready to collapse. Rubble lies strewn about the
ground while deep fissures in the ceiling show the rock is anything but stable.
The walls are covered in pick marks, but they are covered in dust indicating no
one has mined here recently. A steep, rocky path leads down into the darkness.
name: Decaying Mineshaft

desc:
This appears to be the bottom level of an old mine shaft. A steep path leads up
while a dark mining tunnel continues to the south. A few metal hooks in the wall
and some old wooden boards indicate that at one point this shaft was actively
mined. But the thick layer of dust covering every surface reveals this is no
longer the case.
name: Bottom of an Old Mine Shaft

desc:
This passage has been cut through the rock stone by stone with pick and hammer.
Rubble and dirt lie scattered about the passage, making every footstep grind and
crackle. The ceiling is a mix of great cracks and dull brown slate. Several
spots seem likely to fall with the slightest disturbance.
name: Old Mine Tunnel

desc:
This tunnel is a mix of natural rock formations and pick-hewn cavities. A few
small boulders lie about, evidently discarded as worthless by the miners. The
top of the tunnel is bare rock, though large cracks riddle the stone. The dark
passage continues to the north and east.
name: Craggy Tunnel

desc:
A current of trash and excrement fills the centre of this tunnel. It leads east,
toward an opening where the garbage and sewage spill out into the canyon. A
small bit of fresh air from the opening cuts the worst of the vile stench. To
the the west, the line of refuse continues deeper into the earth.
name: Vile Tunnel

desc:
This portion of the tunnel has been fashioned into a disgusting living space. A
few piles of rotting vines have been piled into beds against one wall. In the
centre of the cave, a pot sits on top of a bed of ash. A vile odour emanates
from the pot, it is clearly not fit for consumption by any but the least
discriminating palate.
name: Horrid Lair

desc:
The stench in this cave is overwhelming. A foetid mixture of sweat, excrement,
and rotting food rises from every surface. In one corner, a pile of brown ooze
forms a disgusting bit of decor. A few piles of refuse lay around the room,
clearly set with intention. Though it is unclear why anyone would want such
worthless junk.
name: Malodorous Den

desc:
Both walls of the tunnel are lined with every sort of cast off. Broken
furniture, rotten food, and brown muddy clothing has all been shoved aside to
form a somewhat clear path. Though cleared of the largest debris, the middle of
the tunnel is still filled with a thick muck of mud and worse.
name: Garbage-strewn Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel bends here, which seems to have caused a buildup of caustic sludge.
Along the outer wall, the rock of the tunnel is covered with thick, brown goo.
The air is heavy with a terrible odour that can almost be cut with a knife.
Another smell, just as awful, but different, invades the tunnel from the south.
name: Rancid Passage

desc:
This cave is narrow, and has a pungent aroma that smells like some horrid
combination of bodily waste, turned meat, and fermentation. The source of the
odour is readily apparent. One section of the back wall is entirely covered by
green sludge. About eight feet up, more sludge bubbles out in a slow trickle of
repugnance. Beneath the grime and muck, some some stone arches, carved into the
form of branches, indicate that this tunnel once served a nobler purpose.
name: Foul-smelling Grotto

desc:
This cavern is dominated by dozens of green, oozing pools. They are not uniform
in size. Some look large enough to swallow a bear, while others would barely fit
a boot. Each pool is covered in a film of green algae, which gives the cave its
own distinct odour. Several of the pools have been filled with various objects,
though it is tough to tell what they were originally as many are broken and each
is covered in the same green algae as the pools.
name: Pools of Filth

desc:
This tunnel is narrow, with a brown wet channel of sludge running down the
middle. The channel continues to the east, carrying the sludge away. To the
west, the wall rises in a steep incline. About a hundred feet up the wall, an
opening can be seen around twelve feet in diameter. A brown trail of much covers
the wall below opening, continuing to a pile on the ground below, the source of
the goop.
name: Divided Passage

desc:
Dark and twisted trees form a strangely dense forest. Lichen hangs down from the
limbs. No light reaches the ground, which is covered by a thick layer of rotting
leaves and dead branches. The only visible undergrowth is some fungi and
climbing plants that seem to strangle the trees.
name: Twisted Forest

desc:
If there was once a path through the forbidding forest here, it must get scarce
use these days. Despite there being a discernible space between the black,
twisted trees, that narrow rock-strewn gap is almost entirely obscured by dark,
dense bushes, long thorns and fierce stinging nettles.
name: Dreary, Dwindling Path

desc:
Characterised by massive dark oaks and mutated, twisted beeches, this part of
the dimly-lit forest is a little clearer at ground level than most of the
terrain. Countless years of passing seasons have allowed a dense, decaying mat
of rotting foliage to cover the ground. It is so thick that the thorny, tangled
ground cover seen elsewhere in the forest has been unable to compete. Behind a
large black oak lies a small pit mostly hidden from sight; its contents are
pretty unrecognisable at first glance.
name: Vile Boughs and Misshapen Trees

desc:
The evil blackness of Mirkwood presses in from all sides in a very physical way.
Ancient, twisted trees tangle together so tightly that one is almost
indistinguishable from the next. Undergrowth full of thorns, foul fungi and
decay lines the ground in a continuous mat, and a ceiling of grasping branches
claws down from above.
name: Disfigured Woods

desc:
Thorny shrubs encroach upon the tenuously narrow path from all directions. Their
wicked spikes are razor-sharp and as long as fingers, and every last one appears
hungry to catch and ensnare any who pass by. The path continues to the north and
south, just as fraught with frightful fauna.
name: Wicked, Narrowing Path

desc:
The sharp-edged cliffs prevent movement to the south and east. The whole area is
covered with small cracks, sharp-angled boulders and treacherous gravel. The
lack of plantlife allows the malevolent winds to carry plenty of dust which
makes it difficult to see far into the distance. A very narrow natural bridge
spans over the canyon to the west. To the north the hills become lower, but not
more hospitable.
name: Bare Hill

desc:
A wide span of stone serves as a bridge across a canyon from which a fearsome
stench rises like a invisible, malicious cloud intent on the ruination of any
nose it encounters. The bridge-like span looks to be natural, as it is rough,
uneven, and full of protruding jagged outcroppings that hinder movement.
name: Natural Bridge

desc:
West of a deep canyon, this grey and inhospitable land stretches for miles.
Almost without vegetation and covered by ash-grey dust it looks utterly
lifeless. A small path to the east leads on to a narrow natural bridge.
name: Ashen Hills

desc:
The rocks of this bleak slope are grey and dark like old ashes, stifling all
growth. A rotten smell creeps out of the canyon to the east, as if trying to
escape the confines of the darkness below. The hills rise to the south and the
highest area of Dol Guldur lies some distance to the southwest.
name: Ashen Slope

desc:
These hills are grey and barren, and the few paths are steep and treacherous.
The ground is covered with a loose combination of dust and small gravel that
slips under foot. Ruins of a small structure lie here, the sunken walls are hard
to distinguish from the stones covering the hills.
name: At a Small Ruin

desc:
An ancient wall lies here, shattered and decaying. It is difficult to tell what
the wall was for and who made it, so little of it is left that the stone must
have been taken and used somewhere else after it fell into ruin. The ridge here
is rather high and affords a good view of the bleak surrounding landscape. A
large rock formation is visible in the south east and dark, rolling, treeless
hills persist in the south.
name: Ancient Wall over a Ridge

desc:
Black hills of volcanic stone, littered with sharp-edged rocks surround a small
plateau between a small ridge of hills to the north and a dangerous canyon to
the east. A rough, stone bridge leads east across the canyon toward a massive
spire of rock that stretches into the sky.
name: Hills West of a Stone Bridge

desc:
This narrow space between a rock face to the west and a narrow valley to the
east looks well travelled, despite being nearly a dead end. To the south a sheer
cliff rises defiantly into the air, it's slightly rounded edge looking somewhat
like a bottom jaw. The eastern way into the valley looks severely eroded with
fallen rocks and other debris lying scattered down the narrow entrance. The
slope is very soft and there is clearly no way easy path down.
name: Under a Cliff Face

desc:
This small bridge is built of the same ancient rock as the outer gate fixtures.
A solid wooden door blocks the way to the east. The bridge spans the deep canyon
below which protects the rockspire like a moat. Safety was clearly not a concern
for the builder, without rails or walls, it seems quite possible to fall down
into the canyon below.
name: Stone Bridge

desc:
The land here rises toward the east, the ground covered by dust and loose
gravel. The slopes are filled with cracks making every step uncertain. Some
large boulders form a natural turn, blocking passage to the north and west. To
the east, a flat plateau rises above the forest.
name: Rising Hills

desc:
A narrow ridge connects the black hills below to a bare and windswept plateau.
Steep and treacherous slopes descend to the lower forest to the north and south.
In these directions the brittleness of the ground make the descent an impossible
task. The only safe path runs along the ridge from west to east.
name: Treacherous Ridge

desc:
Bleak and bare hills form this windswept plateau. The forest covering the lower
hills is here replaced by a few struggling brambles and weeds. The plateau
extends in all directions for miles and its edges are sharp and steep. The only
way down seems to be the slope to the west.
name: Bleak, Windswept Plateau

desc:
The bare and flat ground extends in all directions except towards the east where
a sudden drop marks the border of the plateau. There are no trees here, and
vegetation is sparse. The windswept heights hold little topsoil except what's
trapped in small cracks.
name: Edge of a Bleak Plateau

desc:
This plateau rises above the forest to the east and north. Its windswept heights
look down on the dark canopy. The flat surface of the plateau has very little
soil, mostly it is bare, black rock. At one place boulders are piled in a
half-circle, as if someone tried to build a windshelter here, but it looks like
it would provide little shelter.
name: Corner of a Windswept Plateau

desc:
A steep rise ascends from the lower plateau to this higher promontory. Bare,
black rock covers the ground and no plant or tree grow here. The promontory
overlooks the lower forest to the west and to the north, but the canopy is too
dense to see through. Far to the west a rocky outcrop stands out against the sky
with the main part of Dol Guldur beyond it.
name: Rocky Promontory

desc:
The flat plateau is crossed by a shallow ravine filled with brambles and
boulders. Little grows on top of the windswept plateau where the ground is black
rock with barely any soil. The few straggly trees that do grow here are sad
looking things with thin branches and few leaves.
name: At a Ravine on a Plateau

desc:
Some twisted, scraggly birches accompanied by dry, brown bushes are the only
signs of life on this otherwise desolate plateau. The lower land south and east
is covered by a dense forest, but is unreachable due to the steep sides of the
plateau.
name: Bleak, Windswept Plateau

desc:
This cave has been dug directly out of the plateau. The floor is rough stone,
but piles of eroded dirt blur the lines between the walls and the ground. A
hitching post and feed trough line one wall, surrounded by a ring of brown,
dried, droppings. Clearly the occupants are not overly concerned with cleaning.
To the other side, a few logs have been set around a crude fire pit.
name: Hollowed Cave

desc:
This cave is filled with thick, old roots from the forest above. They cover the
entire roof and press in from the sides giving a claustrophobic feel. A few of
the roots have freshly stripped bark, showing that this cave is not uninhabited.
In addition, a packed trail of dirt leads deeper into the cave, away from the
light of the surface.
name: Overgrown Cave

desc:
This is a large cavern, evidently hollowed by some massive creatures. It smells
like some horrible mixture of sweat, unwashed flesh, excrement and the
unmistakable odour of rotting meat. A few large large tree trunks have been
pulled around a pile of burnt bones. A few piles of cloth and leather lay near
the back of the cave.
name: Putrid Warren

desc:
The slopes of these gentle hills are covered with birch and fir, while the
hilltops are dominated by gnarly old oaks. Their crowns are wide and dense,
casting the land into an everlasting darkness. Faint murmurs rustle through the
leaves as the wind stirs them, as if the trees whisper to one another in hushed
voices.
name: Shaded Mirkwood Hillock

desc:
Gentle, rolling hillocks cover the lands in this part of Mirkwood. Slopes,
hilltops and long valleys continue in every direction except toward the west
where a sudden drop puts a halt to any consideration of travel. The sturdy
trees, ancient-looking behemoths, are covered in ensnaring vines and moss in
various shades of green and grey.
name: Slopes of a Mirkwood Hillock

desc:
