The round, softly sloped hills in this area are covered with a dense, strange
looking forest. Most of the trees are oaks that grew tall and strong, but their
bark is almost black. A few ashes and pines stand between the oaks. The
undergrowth is thick, with many thorny climbing vines that conceal some nasty
little cracks in the ground. A sharp edged cliff protects the hills to the west,
while to the east the land changes to a plain forest.
name: Forested Mirkwood Hillocks

desc:
These woods are dark and gloomy, no sky is visible through the ceiling of
leaves. The trees are mostly dark oaks with rough bark and thick branches laden
with leaves. Due to the lack of light, little brush grows under the trees. Dead
leaves cover the ground in a thick layer of rotting plant material, adding an
unpleasant aroma.
name: Sinister Forest

desc:
The ground slopes steeply here, uphill to the east and downhill to the west. Any
movement in other directions is impossible, with a sheer cliff drop to the north
into a pitch-black chasm, and a mass of stinging undergrowth to the south. The
dank forest looms high to the east where even the steep, rock-strewn slope is
filled with the twisted, torturous trees.
name: Rugged Slope

desc:
A huge gate of steel is set in the western wall of this cavern-like room. There
are weapon racks along the walls and chests for supplies, and a small forge to
repair equipment fills one corner of the room. To the south, a passage leads
deeper into the spire.
name: Rough Cavern

desc:
This is clearly a barracks for the spire's defenders. An unpleasant mixture of
sweat and stew gives the room a distinctive odour. Along the walls, rough beds
have been carved directly into the stone, while wooden tables take up the
majority of the floor.
name: Crude Barracks

desc:
This valley is more a cleft in a mountain than a real valley. It is so small
that a goose could jump over it, if a goose would live in these unpleasant
hills. The valley winds in many curves and bends, forming a huge bend from the
south towards the east. The bottom of the valley is littered with many stones
fallen from the walls.
name: Winding Valley between Dark Hills

desc:
The narrow crack, well-concealed by the twisted tree trunks above, widens
quickly to a natural cave and then transforms into a rough-hewn tunnel that
continues to the south. This passageway seems to be very seldom used, judging by
the thick layer of untouched, slimy and slippery crud that covers the wet floor.
The air is musty, stuffy, and moist.
name: Narrow Crack

desc:
A partly collapsed, slightly winding tunnel runs through the cold, wet stone.
The walls are leaning inwards and occasional big rocks have fallen from the
ceiling and from the walls, making travel somewhat difficult. The air is humid
due to numerous streams of black water that trickle between the eroded rocks,
forming deep-looking pools on the floor. At one point, a broad pool stretches
across the whole tunnel, the floor completely hidden beneath the murky black
water.
name: Collapsed, Eroded Tunnel

desc:
Black volcanic stone mixed with dark earth form these hills. To the north, the
cliff is too steep to descend to the lower lands. A few bushes and weeds grow
between the basalt channels, giving a scruffy texture to the otherwise smooth
landscape.
name: Black Hills

desc:
The forest rises to cover a wide but low hill. The trees are old, black oaks in
the lower parts and tall firs higher up. They form a dense ceiling, beneath
which the shadows reign. Little grows here but the ground is covered in dead,
rotting leaves.
name: Deep Forested Hillock

desc:
No light reaches the ground of this forest. The crowns of the trees block the
sunlight completely. The old oaks are black and have a strangeness about them.
Most of them did not grow straight, their trunks are twisted and their roots
seem to wrestle with each other to get the most nutrients from the soil.
name: Looming Trees

desc:
The ground under the dark canopy is soft and muddy. The trees are mostly oaks
and alders that look like they have stood here since an earlier age. The alders
surround a small sinkhole filled with murky, muddy water that smells of sulphur.
The deep tracks of large foot prints leading to the water show that it is
potable for at least some kind of creature.
name: At a Black Forest Pond

desc:
Tall dark trees dominate these rolling hills, their leaf-covered branches
forming an almost solid ceiling above. Little sun ever reaches the ground and as
a result the undergrowth is very sparse. The hills expand in all directions
except to the south, where there is a steep drop.
name: Densely Forested Hillock

desc:
The walls of the tunnel are stricken with claw marks that can be seen leading to
the south and east where they finally disappear into darkness. A foetid smell of
rotting flesh reeks from the southern tunnel.
name: Tunnel of the Hill Trolls

desc:
The tunnel suddenly opens into a large cave that has been turned into a foul
larder. Rotting corpses hang from crude hooks to preserve the meat and prevent
any worms from reaching the flesh. The only way out of here is to head back
north through the tunnel where the air smells slightly cleaner.
name: Larder of the Hill Trolls

desc:
The uneven cavern is crowded from above by low-hanging rock formations. Even the
slightest sounds seem to be magnified, echoing deep into the black void. The
occasional light breeze of fresh air can be felt from the north, but the
dominating atmosphere is damp, dank, and heavy. A few smaller passageways veer
off into the east, but stop abruptly after just a few feet.
name: Black, Echoing Caverns

desc:
A formidable opening in the surrounding black stone gives way to a few major
passageways. Huge piles of crushed and broken rock have been pushed toward the
northern wall, evidently by some beast with unimaginable strength. The ground
here is smooth compared to the sharp and jagged rock walls. The faint sound of
moving water can be heard to the west, while an unpleasant odour wafts in from
the south.
name: At a Toadstool-covered Waypoint

desc:
The cave is narrow here, crowded in from all sides by extremely hard rock. The
moisture in the air has completely covered the passageway, making any hold
extremely slippery and treacherous. Spewing from an unknown source, a small
spring shoots with great force out of the earth. Access to the water is limited
to just a few yards before it is swallowed once again by the solid, black rock.
The sound of the rushing water dominates the air; the closed in surroundings
make the water sound even more powerful than it looks. To the east, the ground
slopes upwards.
name: Underground Tributary

desc:
Huge, entangled tree roots have penetrated the ceiling and the walls of this
rocky, but surprisingly high chamber. The black soil above looks unstable and
volatile, as several loose rocks seem to have shifted with the earth and now
wait patiently for their time to come crashing to the floor below. On the
ground, several somewhat polished rocks and large boulders are looming amid the
thick, white mist that is creeping along the floor.
name: Under the Great, Black Roots

desc:
A large tunnel begins its journey into the dark unknowns under Mirkwood here.
The tunnel is large enough to cause the smallest sound to bounce back and forth
between its walls as it travels deeper underground. The northern end has been
sealed off by a wall of large, solid blocks of stone.
name: At an Ancient Dragon Skeleton

desc:
The trail here is rough, the poisoned foliage cleared just enough to resemble a
path. To the south, the trail leads into a well-worn road. The trail continues
to the east, but above the treetops beyond the trail the massive hill of Dol
Guldur stretches up into sky, crowned by the monstrous fortress of the same
dreaded name.
name: Cruel, Forbidding Path

desc:
Towards the south, there seems to be a little more space between branch and
bough, and less constricting are the twisted vines that tie the corrupted wood
together in this darkened tapestry. Still, there is no natural light, and an
oppressive blanket of darkness hangs heavily upon all it touches. To the north,
the darkness seems to thicken, smothering all it touches. The faint recesses of
old cobblestones laid together is barely visible underfoot.
name: Hidden Path

desc:
The ground here is covered in a thick blanket of rot and decay. Spindly, thin
roots weave together clumps of rotten leaves and moss, binding them together in
a matted layer. Towards the west, the ground begins to become much soggier, with
sickly dew dripping from leaf and stem. Eastward, the twisted flora almost
appears to form a tunnel, as if this was once a path of sorts. To the north, a
similar sight is seen; unwelcoming, twisted and dank lies the surroundings.
name: Hidden Path

desc:
A noxious film of dank-green ooze pocks the standing, stagnant water that
encompasses everything here. Putrid gas bubbles rise towards the surface,
releasing an acrid and malodorous stench as they pop, droplets slowly burning
anything they touch. Gnarled, blackened roots, thick with a dark, putrid ooze,
break through the blanket of rot and decay upon the ground, reaching towards the
virulent bog of poison as if to drink from the corruption that permeates this
place.
name: Dark, Water-logged Forest

desc:
To the west, the bent and mangled flora seems to be slightly less constricting,
with jagged, broken cobblestones barely visible under the matted rot and decay.
Twisted vines, blackened by corruption, hang down as if to ensnare unwary
travellers. To the north, elongated branches wrapped in darkened vines grasp
towards the south.
name: Hidden Path

desc:
To the north, an impenetrable wall of vines, branches, and trunks form a dense
wall of contorted plant life. Towards the west, an almost identical sight
awaits. To the south, the oppressive growth of twisting, interwoven branches and
vines seems to lessen, almost parting as if it was once an established path,
many long, forgotten years ago.
name: Black Pond

desc:
Thick, massive trunks grow tightly together here in all directions, most notably
to the east, where a sickly moss begins to hang heavily the further north and
east one ventures. Blackened lichen and moss drape from branch to branch, as if
the trees are trying to claim any space that could be used for anything other
than their own dark intent.
name: Dark Grove

desc:
Towards the west, a few small openings in the matted layer of decay that lays
heavily upon the ground reveal the once-polished stones of a long-forgotten
pathway. What were once healthy trees, evenly spaced and uniform in plantation,
suggests this was once a meticulously tended grove, likely of Elven origin.
Whatever beauty once lived here, now only death and decay flourishes. What were
once elegant, sprawling branches, are now a twisted visage of gnarled
mortification.
name: Under Gnarled Branches

desc:
Countless gnarled vines and decomposing earthen sinew form a thick blanket upon
the ground here. From within this matted layer of rot and decay, menacing vines,
heavy with barb and thorn, reach from within to wrap themselves around the dark
trunks of these once-proud trees. The thorns, thick with many smaller
protrusions, stab at the bark of these towering and sickly trees, causing thick
lesions from which a black, tarry substance oozes.
name: Dark Grove

desc:
It appears as if evil incarnate itself willed the surrounding area into being.
What were once tall, proud mallorn trees, are now blackened, corrupt pillars
climbing upwards a great distance. Extending from these trunks are gnarled,
ooze-covered boughs and branches, reaching down towards the blackened and
twisted forest floor. From these hang blankets of sickly brown lichen and
corrupted earthen sinew, as if the trees themselves were ominous puppeteers
ready to drape their dark curtains closed upon trespassers.
name: Dark Grove

desc:
The evenly-spaced trunks of what once resembled trees now appear to be nothing
more than dark, corrupted monoliths of black-rot and malice. Much like rest of
this gnarled place, a thick, matted blanket of thorny vines and decomposing
plant matter covers the ground in a thick layer. Massive, low-hanging boughs
droop towards the ground despite their size; heavy is the corruption that
permeates the sheets of putrid decay hanging from them. Blackened vines with
elongated thorns, secreting a putrid, noxious ooze, thrust upwards to wrap
themselves around the rot-laden branches, slowly pulling them downwards as if
arms being bound by an unforgiving rope.
name: Dark Grove

desc:
Towards the north, the gnarled mass of vines and darkened boughs, heavy with a
rank layer of rot, seem to part ever slightly as if a path once existed in that
direction. The ground appears as if it was caught in a roiling, violent
upheaval, as the ground is comprised of deep, muck-laden holes, next to which a
prominent mound thrusts upwards.
name: Rotting Vegetation

desc:
Towards the west, the decay-stricken ground begins to gradually become uneven,
seemingly the cause of numerous sinkholes. Several of these low-lying holes are
filled with a dark, stagnant water. To the north and west, the trees grow
tightly together, with thorny vines weaving between them preventing travel in
those directions. The familiar draping blankets of blackened rot hang in
abundance from the limbs and branches of the once-mighty, now-corrupted trees
here. Several thick branches are snapped and twisted from the sheer volume of
death and decay that has fallen down from the higher branches.
name: Rotten Corner

desc:
The ground here slopes considerably downward from all sides, which are coated in
a slick and sickly layer of dark coloured lichen, forming a deep depression in
the earth. The bottom of this hole contains a thick layer of interwoven vines,
roots, and other decomposing plant matter, held aloft by several inches of
stagnant, murky water. From the sides of the hole spring horn-like thorns,
tripping with a dark, smelly substance that oozes downwards.
name: Soggy Hole

desc:
A few rolling black hills cover the landscape, void of much life and saturated
with the presence of evil. The ground is remarkably hard, almost as if the
entire hillside is made of black stone. Shooting up from the earth to the north
is a steep wall of solid black slate, offset only by the occasional craggy ledge
adorned with sharp, serrated rocks. To the south, the dangerous forest of
Mirkwood looms, as terrifying as ever.
name: Naked Hillside

desc:
The unyielding blanket of darkness and evil that covers the whole forest does
not seem any less thick here. All the plants here, including a dense wall of
stinging nettles to the north, seem to twist in pain in the breeze. All are
tortured and malformed, disease and decay masking their natural shapes.
name: Sullied Forest

desc:
Moving through this dense forest is tough, and is made no easier by the clawing
branches that grasp down from above. One must constantly stoop and duck to toil
onward through the dark, twisting trees. Many strange rustlings and hissings
emanate down from the branches overhead, though it is not clear whether it is
from the trees moving in the wind or from some vile creatures within them. What
looks like impassable debris blocks access north and west. To the south, the
brambles are thick, but passage seems possible.
name: Dark, Sinister Branches

desc:
The cloudy, murky water makes visibility extremely limited. The jagged rock
walls and floor are covered in a slippery, green slime. Visual navigation is
next to impossible due to the dense murk. As the water and anything that is
settled in it is disturbed, visibility becomes even more cumbersome.
name: Submerged Cavern

desc:
Visibility is extremely limited due to the murk of the water. Through the murk,
barely discernible walls can be made out, covered in some green slime. The walls
and floor seem to run off to the west, but the cloudiness of the water makes
anything uncertain.
name: Submerged Cavern

desc:
A massive pile of rubble lies at the end of this passage. Boulders, stones, and
pea-sized rocks all lay together. Evidently this is where the miners left stone
unsuited for anything else. A dank smell emanates from the pile, apparently
there is something wet beyond.
name: Massive Pile of Rubble
